item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Communication via cellphone and radio are possible, but unreliable.

They've sent people down there — and those people have sent back pictures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How could the Foundation have these photos if nobody ever got out and communication with anyone stuck in there is impossible? How would it know that the writing on the phones is in Japanese? There seems to be information in the SCP description that was not obtainable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i can feel the cold.
VOICE 2: Thank you for upvoting Izatova Parking Center. Have a pleasant day.

item.title: Re: Jesus christ.
item.content:encoded: i came here to say basically the same thing. the "i love you" bit gave me all those stockholm syndrome flags at once

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the reasons I changed the numbering of the addendum files in this skip is to imply there's a tape missing (the second audio log). This is to imply that he might have attempted to answer this question in the second tape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this…but….

WHY DIDN'T HE EVER EVEN TRY TO ANSWER HER QUESTION?!

It was said earlier in the comments how it probably wouldn't work. I'd be ok if her response was just to repeat her line ignoring his attempts, but he would start saying number or letters or something…

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don't like that logic. "This thing could be infinite, in which case there could possibly be some sort of connection to that other thing". That's extremely weak.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: proposal rejected by community

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

I propose a crosslink to SCP-413, considering that both of these skips are parking garages that could be infinite.

Editing this:

Although efforts are ongoing to determine the amount of levels below SCP-3074's first floor, evidence suggests the number is well above 100.

To this:

Although efforts are ongoing to determine the amount of levels below SCP-3074's first floor, evidence suggests the number is well above 1001.

Footnotes
1. It is speculated that SCP-3074 may be infinite. If this is true, it is possible that it is related to SCP-413 in some way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think they kept these audio recordings around the office for laughs because this schlemiel never even tried answering the prompt. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well. That's one depressing audio log.

Also, pitch black nights and green lights are insanely creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the fuck why is this so creepy oh my god. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hippo, you son of a bitch.

I'm reading Kafka now and have found myself obsessed with absurdist fiction.

Good job mon ami!

Smashed that +1 button, squadfam!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, what a Kafka-esque parking garage.

this would have been way funnier if, when the guy started ripping off his skin, the voice on the phone said "please, no meat-touching, ma'am!"

but on the real doe, what would happen if the foundation send someone down there into the elevator with an armed nuclear warhead

why didn't you have the guy try to say some random precincts/zones/numbers/letters?

i still liked this story tho but it sort of is a tale pretending to be a scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took a while for this SCP to grow on me, and I think I've figured out two reasons why:

1. Having the audio logs be cassette recordings feels odd, because where did they come from? Why weren't any other victims recorded. Are the offices above the garage where Voice 2 is supposed to be? Unfortunately, I can't think of any alternative way of introducing the logs, which are the best part of the article.

2. Why didn't Voice 1 ever try listing off a few locations, letters, and numbers? There would be an instantaneous stronger connection to the character if, somewhat early on, they desperately tried "appeasing" Voice 2. Even if they never tried again after that.

Either way, I definitely liked it by the end. Parking garages have always felt Escher-like to me, so this was an interesting variation on that feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I HATE looking for parking when I go to IKEA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… What were the audio logs doing in those offices?

*shudders* oh the implications

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Distressingly relatable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The following audio was recovered from compact cassette tapes found in the offices above SCP-3074. All dialogue is translated from Ukrainian.

Emphasis mine. Also notable, because I think people miss it:

Communication via cellphone and radio are possible, but unreliable.

Again, emphasis mine.

Also, it looks like you're using my old user-icon. Weird. o_O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if they can't get back out once they're down there and are cut off from all external contact, how did the foundation get the logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe you should add addendum.
About reclassification as Safe due to easy guarded.
Or about decline such reclassification due to not knowing what is chattering and scuttling in the depths of 100+ floors. And memo from O5, what we shoudn't play "safe" with something you can't understend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! xD Re: Safe vs Euclid —

My head-canon is that it's 'Safe' if the Foundation can contain it without expending any amount of ongoing effort. Since this is a location with an entrance (one which they don't want to plaster with concrete), it has to be guarded; guarding it is super-easy because of its remote location in Pripyat, but it still requires effort.

I could totally see this being designated as 'Safe', though? It seems like it's right on the edge, to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent. Long time ago I was working on a similar garage skip based confusing and impossible arrangements of floors, coupled with the obnoxious distances to be walked between each only to turn the corner to disappointment. I couldn't communicate the experience of getting stuck in there for hours/days/years though, so I loved seeing you capture such a bizarre, slow-onset feeling of loneliness and fatigue.

Shouldn't this be classified as "safe" though? Even with 3074-C unaccounted for, it seems pretty easily secured…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This would've been +1 without setting it in Pripyat; however that did increase the force with which the upvote was delivered.

Referencing Kafka: The Musical in the Author Post made the force increase exponential.

I really would like to see either some D-Class or MTF exploration logs, as I think the evolution of "NOPE" you'd take with it would end up entertaining regardless of direction; but might be redundant to the balance of it as a whole, with how well the dialogue in those audio logs was done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. I'm not sure why he didn't try saying the name of a Precinct or zone, or a letter or number though. Maybe he thought it was some kind of test? Or maybe he was recruited as D-Class for being a Dadaist. Either way, +1. Keep up the good work, you wonderful strange person!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VOICE 2: Thank you for choosing Izatova Parking Center. Have a pleasant day.

This is genius. Definitely a +1 Hippo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hippo, you are a goddam skip machine. Keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great to see the idea's fully realized. It was a pleasure to brainstorm some ideas with you in chat.

Precinct, zone, number or letter/10 and a vigorous +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never read any of Kafka's works but holy shit this was good. As a lot of other people have said I'm glad you made it clear there was a real person behind the phone, really gets the vibe across. The only suggestion I can make is adding more, like maybe having some test logs with D-classes so we can see what else goes on down there, but maybe that's just me (I like diving super-deep into these things and over analyzing them, so I really want to see what happens when you give an answer to the voice).

TL;DR: +1 for creative spooks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: man, another really good one Hippo! I wanted there to be a translation somewhere of what the japanese instructions on the phones say but ~it is a mystery~ +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fucked up shit is that there is no twist. You're just stuck in a parking garage until you're a bug. Oh god this is a metaphor for life

Seriously this is so simple and brilliant and well executed and I love it, just like every single one of the bazillion other articles you've put out lately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Non-Author Non-Canon Nonsense
- Hide

Maybe something came out of it? Some thing so intensely evil, the government tried to destroy it by sabotaging a nearby nuclear power plant.

Maybe it's a test from God. Reach the bottom and you'll find salvation. Get lost on the way and you'll know what eternal damnation is like.

No matter what the canon actually is, someone NEEDS to write a tale about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I gave an upvote, I don't know why I'm expecting some kind of twist or fucked up shit on the final audio log. You know, the kind of thing 3001 did at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised Voice 1 didn't answer the question that voice two 'kept asking. (+1)
Or, i missed something and the foundation tried, because i say any result from doing that would be important because of her *slight* case of repetition, and thus worth recording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that people who go down there can meet with other people who went down there. Don't hold me to that; I think it's also very possible that if you go down there, you'll never see another human being again (even if twenty people went before you and twenty more go after). But in my mind, there's just one parking lot (with numerous entrances — see my post above to Shadow9771).

Re: How far you can go down… That, I have no idea. The Foundation knows there's at least 100 floors — so that tells you how far they managed to get before something stopped them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing about this place: We're told several times there's numerous abandoned cars throughout it (enough to form barricades; enough to search). Okay, that means numerous people have been down here before. But…

Author Head Canon Nonsense
– hide block

…it's in Pripyat, Ukraine. This is a town that was literally only occupied for 20 years before they had to evacuate it (because of Chernobyl). I mean, maybe this parking lot swallowed up a lot of people in 20 years, but you think someone would have noticed, right? Pripyat had a population of 40,000; anymore than 50 people going missing over that time is gonna raise some eyebrows, isn't it? And there's presumably way more than 50 cars down there.

And it was only noticed after the town was evacuated, which makes this even weirder. So, wait, was it not even there while the town was active? So where the hell did all the cars come from, then?

There's one other important clue: The instructions on the phones are written in Japanese.

Maybe the only reason the woman speaks in Ukrainian is because that's what the guy she was talking to spoke. Maybe she knows a lot more languages.

Maybe this parking lot has a lot more entrances..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That makes me ask, if you send 2 people down there together, can they hang?

And how much deeper can they make it, before going insane and removing the itchy skin covering their shiny chitin? Or whatever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's fair, and tbh, I'm kind of surprised this has worked for as many people as it has? I find it terrifying, but for personal reasons; the indifference of others in the face of a desperate need for some sort of human connection is like, one of my two phobias (the other being bugs).

I mean, you could literally describe this story as:

Man goes into parking garage. Man cannot leave. Woman on phone is a jerk and refuses to help. Man descends deeper, ends up turning into some sort of monster (maybe?). THE END

And if that's all that you get from it, I can see how it wouldn't work for you (and I can see how that could be all you get from it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I didn't make this too explicit, but:

SCP-3074-A is a freight elevator used to access the first floor of SCP-3074. Pressing its only button will lower the elevator into SCP-3074. At this point, it becomes impossible to locate its occupants via GPS signal. Communication via cellphone and radio are possible, but unreliable.

Emphasis mine. The Foundation has sent D-Class down there to take pictures. They're presumably still down there (the Foundation sends them food and supplies via the elevator, but they might have stopped sending messages back up long ago).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels like SCP-087 with dashes of SCP-3001 and SCP-847.

My greatest issue is with where the photos came from. I'm assuming it's from the office where they found the tapes, but the fact remains, when you can't look inside a place and take information out, there's no way to even know how it's set up inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hoooooly shit.

NEW FAVORITE SCP WOOOOOOOOOOOO

In all seriousness, this is amazing. Parking garages have always been creepy for me, and the things scuttling about in the dark creates great suspense. The dialogue is amazingly written, and I actually feel bad for the accountant. I think that the elevator is a portal to another world where shit went down and people took refuge in a giant parking center, Metro2033 style.

why can't I +1 this more than once

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

I think I'm in a distinct minority of one here, but this didn't do much for me. It feels a bit like a retread of old ideas when it comes to exploration logs, without adding anything to the mix. It's well-executed but a little hollow; the emotional crunch never really takes place for me. The fact that it's a human on the other side doesn't give me a sense of horror; it seems more like obvious and mindless sadism. There's no reluctance, no human emotion being displayed, just occasional details to lift it from being purely automatic.

Reluctant -1. It's very well executed (as ever) but too hollow.

EDIT JANUARY 2018: What the ever-loving hell was past-me thinking, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant as always. Of course it's Hippo that can make a parking garage into effective horror.

~ Glory to Arstotska ~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Also, yeah, I was worried about this piece coming off as heavily derivative of 3001 (as it kind of veered into that direction), but I was hoping that by making it clear that the person on the other side was a human being (thanks to tretter for suggesting playing that up more) I could get a different sort of horror across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely brilliant. In 3001, while Scranton did desparately tried to talk to Red, deep down he knew it can't talk. In this case, knowing that behind the voice is a human (or something similar) but can't talk to them and instead receiving a sentence repeated over and over, it must be terrifying. Also, I love how the occasional sighs and yawns give so much character to what most would assume as an automated message. Amazing work as always, Hippo.

item.title: in my headcanon
item.content:encoded:
VOICE 1: Precinct 5! Zone M!

VOICE 2: I'm sorry, that area is outside our jurisdiction. Please hold while we transfer you to the appropriate division.

Hold music plays for 141 days, 13 hours, and 22 minutes.

OR

VOICE 1: Precinct 5! Zone M!

VOICE 2: Thank you for calling. However, due to technical issues, this request must be processed in person. Please proceed to the ticket office, located on the [UNINTELLIGIBLE]th, bottom floor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Years ago, when public payphones were everywhere, I walked past one that was ringing. I was momentarily tempted to pick it up and say, "Sector Q, Nine speaking."

Haven't thought of that in a while. Great Hippo, that hard left into terror-town was. hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me imagines the Foundation sending a D-Class down there with a laptop running vocal sample software that will iterate through every feasibly imaginable type of 'Precinct', 'Zone', and 'number/letter' in every imaginable combination until they get an interesting result.

I'm not sure if that would work. My gut says that if it did, it would take a couple of decades — after which the Foundation would, at last, obtain a validated parking spot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VOICE 2: Precinct, zone, number or letter.

VOICE 1: Precinct 5! Zone M!

VOICE 2: … oh! Okay, we'll send someone down to get you. Just wait there.

item.title: Re: Jesus christ.
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate it! For me, this is one of the most terrifying things: Not just being at the mercy of human indifference, but being so desperate for contact that you'll do anything to appease those who just don't care. You'll strip yourself of every last shred of dignity, just for the chance that you'll be acknowledged — that you'll gain some tiny mote of approval. Humans are social creatures; without relationships, we starve. And when you're starving, you'll eat anything. Even yourself.

It's a place I've been a couple of times. I think I've moved beyond it (I hope!), but I'm at least familiar with that sort of head-space, and it still scares the crap out of me to think about it. I was trying to channel it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. So fucking good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the description of hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderfully creepy and kafkaeqsue in all the right ways.

+1

item.title: Amazing
item.content:encoded: This was Incredibly well written, the way you added a personality tothe voice just added another level to the detail that you don't get very often, well played!

item.title: Jesus christ.
item.content:encoded: Was that ever a punch to the fucking gut. God. I thought it was gonna be a reference to The Trial and it was and then… oh god. Also when he starts saying "I love you." Man. Jesus fuck. How you assign a personality to that voice. The callousness in the denial of real contact.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
HE IS FRANZ KAFKA
(FRANZ KAFKA)
BE CAREFUL IF YOU GET HIM PISSED
(FRANZ KAFKA)
HE'LL SMITE YOU WITH METAPHOR FISTS

SOURCES:

  • Images from here and here.
  • Inspired by Papers, Please, The Metamorphosis, The Enigma of Amigara Fault, and that one time I got stuck in a parking garage where every space was empty and reserved.

FEEDBACK:

  • Taffeta - ([SCP-3878] - Words Will Never Hurt Me)
  • DrChandra - ([SCP-3806] - I AM AWAITED IN VALHALLA!)
  • weryllium - ([SCP-3637] - Many Waters Cannot Quench Love, Nor Can The Floods Drown It)
  • tretter - ([SCP-2579] - Grant's Bears)
  • communism will win - ([SCP-3406] - Awakening)
  • Boogeyman23 - (Charcoal)
  • Goodeveningdrwhite
  • Aoneareyou60

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
